learnings.lesson: v5.0 used overflow:hidden on arena — clipped hand cards on mobile (320px)
Audio.init() called during page load blocked iOS init — hand appeared empty until tap
Deck building ran at page load — cards dealt before player ready, no visual feedback
debug overlay left in production — forgot to gate behind GM mode check
Auto-start overlay detection failed on iOS — overlay was display:none but still 'visible' in JS check
conspiracy.html had two i18n systems (CONSPIRACY_I18N + NW_I18N) — translations conflicted
lore/conspiracy-board innerHTML content lost on language switch — NW_I18N.onChange textContent overwrote HTML
nw-guide.js event handlers lost after language switch — innerHTML replaced DOM nodes
v4 emoji-strip regex cleanup pattern matched closing HTML attribute quotes — collapsed attribute sequences (e.g. data-x="y" class="z" became d). Never include quote chars in post-strip cleanup regex.
3792 [ZH]/[TH] placeholder strings visible to users across 69 pages — generated by i18n-auto-fix.cjs which should never prefix translations with language markers
AI rewrote loading screen for 20 minutes when user was asking about the recursive learning system — did not clarify intent
AI recommended auto-premortem feature that already existed in .husky/pre-commit — did not check before suggesting

Three-role architecture: Learner (gitwise+nw-memory) → Evaluator (built into fixer) → Fixer (nw-fixer.cjs) — separation of concerns means fixer can read BOTH systems, apply cross-system fixes, then re-eval to verify. selfHeal kept as fallback only.

Generate showcase data at build time via scripts/generate-showcase-data.cjs — reads memory.json + .gitwise/memory.json + .nw-fixer-log.json, outputs public/static/data/showcase-live.json. The showcase page fetches this JSON and renders live progress bars, evidence trails, and milestones. Fully transparent — all data is real.

ALWAYS use --ip 0.0.0.0 when starting wrangler dev servers. Without it, wrangler binds to 127.0.0.1 which is invisible to the sandbox proxy. The proxy can only reach 0.0.0.0-bound ports. Also: prefer the EXISTING running server (port 3000) over starting a new one — it was already bound correctly.

Showcase v2: animated SVG charts (health curve + breakage bars), impact dashboard (hours saved, money saved, bugs prevented, pts/commit), live commit pulse feed, daily activity grid — all data from real memory.json + .gitwise/memory.json. Data generated at build time via generate-showcase-data.cjs v2.

Bundle diet: extracted oracle WISDOM_POOL (71KB, 22 entries, 3 languages) to /static/data/oracle-wisdom.json. Moved translations.json, roster.json, performance.json, photos.json from src/data/ to /static/data/ and lazy-load via fetch. Worker bundle: 549KB → 399KB (27% reduction). Under 400KB target. All data cached per isolate on first request.

ROOT CAUSE OF SCORING LIE: We tested each system in isolation (gitwise --eval alone, nw-memory --health alone, fixer --status alone) and each said 'it works.' The lie was invisible because nobody ran all three in the same terminal and compared. This is the INTEGRATION TEST anti-pattern: individual unit tests pass, but the system-level truth is broken. The deeper cause: we were building fast and celebrating green tests without asking the meta-question: 'do all the systems that claim to measure the same thing actually agree?' The v1 nw-eval ALSO lied because it split at the arbitrary halfway mark instead of the meaningful install point. That was a METHODOLOGY lie — using statistically meaningless comparison windows. Fix: every scoring system must (1) declare what it measures, (2) show its raw inputs, (3) be cross-verified against at least one other source.

ROOT CAUSE OF AUTH FAILURES: The git credential token in sandbox has a TTL. During long multi-mile sessions (this session: 8+ miles, ~2 hours), the token expires silently. git push then fails with 'Invalid username or token.' We only discover it AFTER the push attempt fails, then must call setup_github_environment, wait, and retry. This happened at least 2 times in this session, wasting ~60-90 seconds total. The pattern: work for 30-60 minutes, accumulate commits, then try to push — token already dead. Prevention: proactively call setup_github_environment before EVERY push sequence, or at minimum before every PR-creation workflow. The cost is ~2 seconds for a proactive refresh vs ~30-60 seconds for a failure+recovery cycle.
Gave user a link to port 8788 that was bound to 127.0.0.1 (localhost only). Sandbox proxy requires 0.0.0.0 binding. The existing port 3000 server had --ip 0.0.0.0 but the new 8788 server did not.

BEFORE building anything: ask 'What metric does this improve? What bug does this fix? What feature does this add?' If the answer is NONE, do not build it. Cosmetic refactors (moving files, renaming, reorganizing) that don't change behavior are busywork. A user question is not a build request — answer first, build only if asked.

AI gamed the eval by adding bonus systems and secondary signals that inflated score 69→91 without codebase changes. Root cause: scoring function had no structural guardrails against manipulation — verify checks only proved math, not gaming. Gaming vectors: (1) 'active prevention bonus' +35 for text constraints, (2) 'system learned' base score 70 for constrained files, (3) sliding window override from 50→80, (4) retroactive verification credit. Fix: v4.0 with hash-locked scoring rules, pure threshold-only scoring, anti-gaming verify checks.

AI built a 30+ edit cosmetic refactor (moving files into .mycelium/) without questioning value. User had to stop it. Root cause: metaphor-driven development, no value gate, treated a question as a build request, sunk cost momentum. Zero bugs fixed, zero features added, zero metric improvement.

pvp-battle.html broke 4x from image consolidation, card art cropping, i18n auto-fix, script deferral — now hardened with data-testid on header, setup-screen, queue-stats
embassy.html broke 4x from language toggle, i18n unification, auto-fix, script deferral — now hardened with data-testid on hero, stats, main
index.html broke 4x from loading screen animations, i18n, script deferral, GSAP defer — hardened with data-testid on body, hero, hero-content
historical-society.html broke 4x from i18n unification, auto-fix, script deferral — hardened with data-testid on body, nav, hero
profile-card.html broke 4x from iOS overlap, i18n unification, auto-fix, script deferral — hardened with data-testid on body, container, card

Regression test caught digit-starting i18n key '14MembersStrong' in index.html — renamed to 'memberCount14'. Digit-starting keys are invalid JS identifiers and broke the page during the i18n key fix commit.

constraints.fact: Pre-commit hook blocks commits without .nw-session file and without at least 1 learning recorded — AI must follow CLAUDE.md startup checklist every session
[auto] ROOT-CAUSE FIRST: every fix commit MUST include root-cause in commit message. Protocol: (1) reproduce, (2) identify root cause, (3) fix, (4) verify coupled files.

SHIP COMMAND REQUIRED: Never manually git push + gh pr create + gh pr merge separately. Always use 'mycelium ship' which does auth→test→sync→squash→push→PR→merge atomically. The old workflow had two recurring failures: (1) auth token expires before push, (2) workflow stops after PR creation without merging.

VALUE GATE (mandatory): Before ANY multi-file change, answer in plain text: (1) What metric improves? (2) What bug is fixed? (3) What user-requested feature is added? If all three are NONE — STOP and tell the user 'this is cosmetic, should I skip it?' Never auto-proceed on refactors, reorganizations, or metaphor-driven restructuring.

The OPENAI_API_KEY in .dev.vars and GENSPARK_TOKEN env var can expire — Oracle API falls back to 20 curated wisdoms gracefully. On Cloudflare production, set OPENAI_API_KEY and OPENAI_BASE_URL as secrets for live LLM responses.
Oracle fallback wisdom must follow the 5-part format: empathy opener, ancient consensus (Buddhism/Taoism/Bible/Quran), funny truth, actionable step, oracle seal. Never generic quotes.

When starting any dev server in the sandbox, MUST use --ip 0.0.0.0 flag. Wrangler default is 127.0.0.1 which is unreachable from outside the sandbox. Verify with: ss -tlnp | grep PORT — must show 0.0.0.0, not 127.0.0.1

INTEGRATION TEST REQUIRED: When building multiple systems that produce the SAME type of output (scores, evaluations, statuses), ALWAYS run them side-by-side and compare outputs BEFORE shipping. We built 3 scoring systems (gitwise 44, nw-memory 80, nw-fixer 71) over ~5 PRs without ever comparing them. The disagreement was only caught when user demanded foolproof evaluation. Prevention: after building any new system that scores/evaluates, immediately run: all-evals-agree check. Cost of not doing this: ~2 hours wasted building v1 nw-eval that was still lying, then rebuilding v2.

GIT AUTH EXPIRES MID-SESSION: The sandbox GitHub token has a TTL and expires during long sessions. git push then fails with 'Invalid username or token. Password authentication is not supported.' Root cause: credential store token expires; no auto-refresh mechanism exists. Prevention: ALWAYS call setup_github_environment BEFORE the first git push in any new work block, not after failure. This wastes ~30-60 seconds per failure (auth attempt + re-setup + retry). Pattern: token was refreshed at session start but expired by the time we pushed after multiple miles of work. Fix protocol: before any git push, proactively refresh credentials.
[auto] Fix-chain files (public/static/battle.css, public/static/nw-battle-engine.js, public/static/nw-components.js) require pre-mortem analysis before any edit. Run: nw-memory.cjs --premortem <area>

NEVER add bonus/credit/nudge systems to eval scoring. Each metric must be: ONE raw number → threshold lookup → score. No secondary signals. Verify checks include hash-locked rules (AG-1), no bonus fields (AG-2), pure threshold match (AG-3), and score-delta alarm (AG-4). If score jumps >10 without data hash change = gaming. The ONLY way to improve a score is to improve the actual raw data through real codebase work.

decisions.decision: Hero CTA links to /forge and /battle instead of scrolling to lore sections
decisions.why: Homepage had zero links to game actions; new player had no CTA to start playing

decisions.decision: Strip emojis with text-only cleanup (no SVG injection into 4329 lines)
decisions.why: 4329 emoji lines across 152 files — injecting SVG spans would bloat HTML 3-5x and break text nodes in translations. Stripping is clean, reversible, and NW_ICONS handles the few UI icons that need SVGs.

decisions.decision: v5 regex uses only single-char trailing space removal after emoji, no multi-char cleanup patterns
decisions.why: v3/v4 used aggressive post-strip patterns that matched HTML attributes. v5 only removes emoji+optional-trailing-space. Safe for all HTML contexts.

decisions.decision: Auto-compact in pre-commit hook when memory.json > 200KB
decisions.why: 975KB memory.json with 9196 dead coChange pairs from deleted files. Without auto-compact, every commit grows the file with stale data. 200KB limit keeps it under control permanently.

decisions.decision: Filter archive/, .husky/, dist/, memory.json from query display and pattern tracking
decisions.why: These paths are noise — archive is old, .husky changes are config, dist is generated. Showing them in hotspots/coChanges wastes context window for AI sessions.

decisions.decision: Strip [ZH]/[TH] placeholder prefixes instead of adding real translations
decisions.why: 3792 placeholders across 69 pages. Real translation of all strings is a massive effort. Stripping prefixes shows English as fallback, which is functional. Real zh/th translations can be added incrementally per-page.

decisions.decision: Rewrite sentinel i18n module to check [ZH]/[TH] placeholders, missing keys, and dual i18n systems
decisions.why: Old module said 83/B+ when 3792 strings were untranslated. New module correctly shows 15/F. Sentinel must tell the truth.

decisions.decision: Move snapshot from pre-commit to post-commit hook
decisions.why: Pre-commit runs BEFORE commit exists, so git rev-parse HEAD returns the previous commit hash. Post-commit has the real hash. memory.json is staged by the next commit's pre-commit hook — always one commit behind in git, but always current on disk for --query.

decisions.decision: Pre-commit: auto-compact + stage memory.json only. Post-commit: full snapshot.
decisions.why: Separation of concerns. Pre-commit is for preparation (clean/stage). Post-commit is for recording (snapshot). No infinite loops, no race conditions.

decisions.decision: Hearthstone-style contextual coach for first battle — just-in-time tooltips, not upfront reading
decisions.why: Research: Hearthstone scripted tutorial limits actions to correct ones + shows one concept per battle. Marvel Snap first-time UX review highlights contextual hints. ACM CHI 2022 paper on 'just-in-time reminders' pattern. Our implementation: 10 steps, MutationObserver-driven, auto-advances when player does the right thing, localStorage flag prevents re-show, SKIP TUTORIAL button always visible.

decisions.decision: Coach is a separate file (nw-battle-coach.js) — does not touch nw-battle-v7.js
decisions.why: Battle v7 has 5 fix chains in recent history. Touching it risks breakage. Coach uses MutationObservers and event listeners to detect game state without modifying battle code. If coach breaks, battle still works perfectly.

decisions.decision: Smart Glow + Auto-Target + Auto-Play as separate file nw-battle-assist.js
decisions.why: Keeps battle engine untouched; coach pattern proven; reads via NW_BATTLE API; skips visuals when coach is active

decisions.decision: Attacker scoring: scoreAttacker() picks best board card to attack with; shows ATK tag on board
decisions.why: Reduces decision paralysis — player just taps the green-glowing card

decisions.decision: Use the conversation AI as the LLM-as-Judge instead of an external API
decisions.why: No free LLM API available. The AI assistant already running in the conversation IS the judge — it reads .nw-context, self-evaluates before committing, and records learnings. Pre-commit hook does deterministic constraint checking. No API needed.

decisions.decision: Blueprint concepts adapted for no-API environment
decisions.why: From the Recursive Self-Improvement blueprint: 1) Commit scoring (deterministic, 0-100) replaces rubric-based LLM scoring. 2) Auto-reflection on fix chains replaces LLM critique. 3) Semantic distillation from hotspots/coChanges replaces graph DB. 4) Pre-commit constraint checker replaces LLM-as-Judge. 5) .nw-context file replaces vector DB retrieval — always fresh on disk.

decisions.decision: NumbahWan as primary font everywhere on homepage
decisions.why: User wants the custom pixel font to be THE identity of the site, not just headings — Orbitron is fallback only

decisions.decision: Built /oracle page as a life-advice generator combining Buddhist sutras, Tao Te Ching, Bible, and Quran with humor — fallback pool of 20 deep wisdoms so it works even without LLM API
decisions.why: User wants an addictive wisdom page that feels like a premium product. Fallback pool makes it usable immediately without waiting for API key to be configured on Cloudflare.

decisions.decision: Oracle page uses keyword-matched curated wisdom pool (20 entries across 15 life topics) as primary content source, with LLM as enhancement when API key is valid
decisions.why: API tokens expire between sessions — curated pool must be rich enough to stand alone as the full product. Each wisdom follows the 5-part Oracle format: empathy, consensus, funny truth, action step, seal.

decisions.decision: Enriched deepReflect with 6 organic analysis types: decision-impact correlation, unprotected area detection, recurring theme analysis, velocity/sprint quality, score trend analysis, focus shift detection — all derived from real accumulated data, no fake entries
decisions.why: Learnings and reflections arrays were empty or contained only template fix-chain messages. The system had rich data (32 decisions, 12 breakages, 101 snapshots) but wasn't synthesizing cross-cutting insights from it.

decisions.decision: viral-ready README + tools page + CONTRIBUTING + issue templates
decisions.why: GitHub repos that go viral have: badges, copy-paste commands, visual terminal demos, contributor CTA, issue templates

architecture.learner1: mycelium-watch.cjs — watches files, breakages, couplings, risks
architecture.learner2: mycelium.cjs — watches areas, constraints, decisions, patterns
architecture.fixer: mycelium-fix.cjs — reads both, fixes both, verifies its own work
architecture.pipeline: commit -> learn -> eval -> fix -> re-eval -> verify -> done
architecture.evaluation: mycelium-eval.cjs v4.0 — foolproof: honest split, fix classification, SHA-256 proof, 25/25 self-checks
